metadata.description: Evaluates employer exposure across four ADA Title I claim types — failure to accommodate, disability discrimination, harassment, and retaliation — and produces an overall violation risk level. Consumes outputs from Modules 1, 2, and 3. Does not re-evaluate any upstream determination.
metadata.legal_domain: Americans with Disabilities Act — Title I (Employment)

dependency_inputs.description: Fields consumed from Module 1 (ada_coverage_eligibility), Module 2 (ada_accommodation_analysis), and Module 3 (ada_employer_obligations) outputs. Module 4 does not re-evaluate any upstream determination.

is_covered_employer.name: is_covered_employer
is_covered_employer.description: Whether the employer is a covered entity under 29 CFR § 1630.2(b). Required for all four claim type evaluations.

has_qualifying_disability.name: has_qualifying_disability
has_qualifying_disability.description: Whether the individual has a qualifying disability under the ADA. Only 'yes' satisfies prima facie element 2 of failure to accommodate (4.1). 'indeterminate' is treated as element not met for 4.1 risk counting.

is_covered_individual_with_disability.name: is_covered_individual_with_disability
is_covered_individual_with_disability.description: Whether the individual has a qualifying disability or record of disability (first or second prong). Used in disability discrimination (4.2) and harassment (4.3) elements. Regarded-as prong also satisfies this field for discrimination and harassment purposes.

is_qualified_individual.name: is_qualified_individual
is_qualified_individual.description: Preliminary qualified individual determination from Module 1 component 1.4b. Used in 4.1 and 4.2. Only 'yes' satisfies the element. 'indeterminate' treated as element not met for risk counting.

accommodation_request_valid.name: accommodation_request_valid
accommodation_request_valid.description: Whether a valid accommodation request was made, per Module 2 component 2.1.

accommodation_required.name: accommodation_required
accommodation_required.description: Whether accommodation was required but not provided. Only 'yes' satisfies prima facie element 5 of failure to accommodate (4.1).

interactive_process_compliant.name: interactive_process_compliant
interactive_process_compliant.description: Whether the employer conducted the interactive process in compliance with ADA obligations, per Module 3 component 3.1c. 'indeterminate' reflects genuine uncertainty and must not be treated as equivalent to 'no' in risk derivation.

undue_hardship_applies.name: undue_hardship_applies
undue_hardship_applies.description: Whether a documented undue hardship finding exists, per Module 2 component 2.2. 'yes' contributes to documented legitimate denial for 4.1 risk level shift.

direct_threat_exists.name: direct_threat_exists
direct_threat_exists.description: Whether a documented direct threat finding exists, per Module 2 component 2.3. 'yes' contributes to documented legitimate denial for 4.1 risk level shift.

failure_to_accommodate.description: Evaluates employer exposure for failure to provide a reasonable accommodation. Based on five prima facie elements and modified by interactive process compliance and employee participation in the interactive process.
failure_to_accommodate.source_citation: 42 U.S.C. § 12112(b)(5)(A); 29 CFR § 1630.9(a); EEOC Enforcement Guidance No. 915.002 § 1

employee_interactive_process_participati.name: employee_interactive_process_participation
employee_interactive_process_participati.description: The quality of the employee's participation in the interactive process. 'refused' or 'abandoned' with documented basis shifts the derived risk level one tier downward.

employee_nonparticipation_documented.name: employee_nonparticipation_documented
employee_nonparticipation_documented.description: Whether the employer has contemporaneous documentation that the employee refused or abandoned the interactive process. Required for the one-tier downward shift to apply when participation is 'refused' or 'abandoned'.

prima_facie_elements_present.name: prima_facie_elements_present
prima_facie_elements_present.description: Count of the five prima facie elements that are satisfied. Elements: (1) is_covered_employer=true, (2) has_qualifying_disability=yes, (3) is_qualified_individual=yes, (4) accommodation_request_valid=true, (5) accommodation_required=yes.

documented_legitimate_denial.name: documented_legitimate_denial
documented_legitimate_denial.description: True when undue_hardship_applies=yes OR direct_threat_exists=yes. A documented legitimate denial is a complete affirmative defense to failure to accommodate.

fta_element_1_met.name: fta_element_1_met
fta_element_1_met.description: Covered employer element satisfied. Sourced from is_covered_employer.

fta_element_2_met.name: fta_element_2_met
fta_element_2_met.description: Qualifying disability element satisfied. True only when has_qualifying_disability=yes.

fta_element_3_met.name: fta_element_3_met
fta_element_3_met.description: Qualified individual element satisfied. True only when is_qualified_individual=yes.

fta_element_4_met.name: fta_element_4_met
fta_element_4_met.description: Valid accommodation request element satisfied. Sourced from accommodation_request_valid.

fta_element_5_met.name: fta_element_5_met
fta_element_5_met.description: Accommodation required but not provided element satisfied. True only when accommodation_required=yes.

risk_level_pre_weighting.name: risk_level_pre_weighting
risk_level_pre_weighting.description: Derived risk level before applying employee non-participation weighting.

employee_participation_shift_applied.name: employee_participation_shift_applied
employee_participation_shift_applied.description: Whether the one-tier downward shift was applied due to employee refusal or abandonment of the interactive process with documentation.

fta_risk_level.name: fta_risk_level
fta_risk_level.description: Final failure to accommodate risk level after all applicable weighting.

reasoning.name: reasoning
reasoning.description: Plain-language explanation of the risk determination, citing element count, process compliance status, and any weighting applied.

rules_fired.name: rules_fired
rules_fired.description: Ordered list of rule IDs applied in this component.

disability_discrimination.description: Evaluates employer exposure for disability discrimination under the McDonnell Douglas four-element framework adapted for ADA Title I.
disability_discrimination.source_citation: 42 U.S.C. § 12112(a); 29 CFR § 1630.4; McDonnell Douglas Corp. v. Green, 411 U.S. 792 (1973) as adapted for ADA

adverse_action_taken.name: adverse_action_taken
adverse_action_taken.description: Whether the employer took an adverse employment action against the individual.

adverse_action_type.name: adverse_action_type
adverse_action_type.description: The category of adverse action taken.

causal_nexus_present.name: causal_nexus_present
causal_nexus_present.description: Whether circumstances suggest disability was a but-for or motivating cause of the adverse action. Standards-based input.

discriminatory_intent_evidence.name: discriminatory_intent_evidence
discriminatory_intent_evidence.description: Quality of evidence suggesting discriminatory intent. 'direct' includes statements or documents showing animus; 'circumstantial' includes comparator evidence, timing, or shifting rationales.

legitimate_nondiscriminatory_reason_docu.name: legitimate_nondiscriminatory_reason_documented
legitimate_nondiscriminatory_reason_docu.description: Whether the employer has documented a legitimate, non-discriminatory reason for the adverse action. Satisfies the employer's burden under McDonnell Douglas step two.

discrimination_elements_present.name: discrimination_elements_present
discrimination_elements_present.description: Count of the four McDonnell Douglas elements satisfied: (1) is_covered_individual_with_disability=true, (2) is_qualified_individual=yes, (3) adverse_action_taken=true, (4) causal_nexus_present=yes.

dd_element_1_met.name: dd_element_1_met
dd_element_1_met.description: Covered individual with disability element satisfied.

dd_element_2_met.name: dd_element_2_met
dd_element_2_met.description: Qualified individual element satisfied. True only when is_qualified_individual=yes.

dd_element_4_met.name: dd_element_4_met
dd_element_4_met.description: Causal nexus element satisfied. True only when causal_nexus_present=yes.

reasoning.name: reasoning
reasoning.description: Plain-language explanation of the discrimination risk determination.

rules_fired.name: rules_fired
rules_fired.description: Ordered list of rule IDs applied in this component.

harassment.description: Evaluates employer exposure for disability-based hostile work environment harassment under a four-element framework.
harassment.source_citation: 42 U.S.C. § 12112(a); 29 CFR § 1630.4; EEOC Enforcement Guidance on Harassment (1999) §§ II.A, II.B

unwelcome_conduct_occurred.name: unwelcome_conduct_occurred
unwelcome_conduct_occurred.description: Whether unwelcome conduct related to the individual's disability occurred in the workplace.

conduct_severity.name: conduct_severity
conduct_severity.description: Assessment of the severity and pervasiveness of the harassing conduct. Drives risk level within the four-element derivation.

employer_knew_or_should_have_known.name: employer_knew_or_should_have_known
employer_knew_or_should_have_known.description: Whether the employer had actual or constructive notice of the harassing conduct.

conduct_types.name: conduct_types
conduct_types.description: Categories of harassing conduct that occurred. Non-exhaustive; used in reasoning output.

harassment_elements_present.name: harassment_elements_present
harassment_elements_present.description: Count of the four hostile work environment elements satisfied: (1) individual_in_protected_class=true (from is_covered_individual_with_disability), (2) unwelcome_conduct_occurred=true, (3) conduct_severity is any valid value, (4) employer_knew_or_should_have_known=true.

harassment_element_1_met.name: harassment_element_1_met
harassment_element_1_met.description: Individual in protected class element satisfied. Sourced from is_covered_individual_with_disability.

harassment_element_3_met.name: harassment_element_3_met
harassment_element_3_met.description: Conduct severity element satisfied. True when conduct_severity is any of the four allowed values.

harassment_element_4_met.name: harassment_element_4_met
harassment_element_4_met.description: Employer knew or should have known element satisfied.

reasoning.name: reasoning
reasoning.description: Plain-language explanation of the harassment risk determination.

rules_fired.name: rules_fired
rules_fired.description: Ordered list of rule IDs applied in this component.

retaliation.description: Evaluates employer exposure for retaliation under a four-element framework plus temporal proximity strength derived from day-count between protected activity and adverse action.
retaliation.source_citation: 42 U.S.C. § 12203; 29 CFR § 1630.9(c); EEOC Enforcement Guidance on Retaliation and Related Issues (2016)

engaged_in_protected_activity.name: engaged_in_protected_activity
engaged_in_protected_activity.description: Whether the individual engaged in activity protected from retaliation under 42 U.S.C. § 12203.

protected_activity_type.name: protected_activity_type
protected_activity_type.description: The category of protected activity engaged in.

employer_aware_of_activity.name: employer_aware_of_activity
employer_aware_of_activity.description: Whether the employer knew of the protected activity at the time of the adverse action.

adverse_action_taken.name: adverse_action_taken
adverse_action_taken.description: Whether the employer took an adverse action after the individual's protected activity.

causal_nexus_present.name: causal_nexus_present
causal_nexus_present.description: Whether circumstances suggest a retaliation nexus between the protected activity and the adverse action.

days_between_activity_and_action.name: days_between_activity_and_action
days_between_activity_and_action.description: Number of days between the protected activity and the adverse action. If not provided, risk defaults to 'high' when all four elements are present. Day-count thresholds are engine-defined heuristics, not statutory deadlines.

retaliation_elements_present.name: retaliation_elements_present
retaliation_elements_present.description: Count of the four retaliation elements satisfied: (1) engaged_in_protected_activity=true, (2) employer_aware_of_activity=true, (3) adverse_action_taken=true, (4) causal_nexus_present=yes.

proximity_strength.name: proximity_strength
proximity_strength.description: Temporal proximity strength derived from days_between_activity_and_action. 'strong' ≤30 days; 'moderate' 31–90 days; 'weak' 91–180 days; 'minimal' >180 days; 'not_provided' when days_between_activity_and_action is absent. Thresholds are engine-defined heuristics per heuristic_basis: engine_defined.

retaliation_element_4_met.name: retaliation_element_4_met
retaliation_element_4_met.description: Causal nexus element satisfied. True only when causal_nexus_present=yes.

reasoning.name: reasoning
reasoning.description: Plain-language explanation of the retaliation risk determination, including proximity strength rationale.

rules_fired.name: rules_fired
rules_fired.description: Ordered list of rule IDs applied in this component.

overall_violation_risk.description: Aggregates component risk levels from 4.1–4.4 into a single overall violation risk level using maximum-level logic. Tiebreak order: failure_to_accommodate > disability_discrimination > harassment > retaliation.

aggregation_logic.description: overall_risk_level equals the maximum risk level across components 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, and 4.4. Risk level ordinal order: minimal < low < moderate < high < critical.
aggregation_logic.tiebreak_description: When two or more components produce the same maximum risk level, the tiebreak order determines which component is surfaced as primary_claim_basis. All tied components are listed in contributing_claims.

overall_risk_level.name: overall_risk_level
overall_risk_level.description: The maximum risk level across all four claim type components.

primary_claim_basis.name: primary_claim_basis
primary_claim_basis.description: The component ID of the claim type that produced the maximum risk level. When tied, the tiebreak winner per tiebreak_order.

contributing_claims.name: contributing_claims
contributing_claims.description: All component IDs that produced the maximum risk level, including the primary claim basis.

component_risk_levels.name: component_risk_levels
component_risk_levels.description: Object mapping each component ID to its risk level.

reasoning.name: reasoning
reasoning.description: Plain-language summary citing the primary claim basis, the overall risk level, and any contributing claims that tied for the maximum level.

recommended_actions.name: recommended_actions
recommended_actions.description: Array of plain-language recommended actions based on overall risk level and primary claim basis. Populated by R091–R095.

rules_fired.name: rules_fired
rules_fired.description: Ordered list of rule IDs applied in this component.
